Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and mildly high ALT, indicating possible liver involvement. Triglycerides are slightly elevated, which may increase cardiovascular risk. Low globulin, total bilirubin, and alkaline phosphatase are noted but of unclear clinical significance. Other parameters are within normal limits. Correlation with clinical findings and further evaluation of renal and liver function are recommended.
